Details: Results of one study suggest photocatalytic oxidation of dichlorvos may be effective in drinking water applications. A reactor at pH 4 with titanium dioxide suspensions at 254 nm was found to be more effective in the photocatalytic oxidation of dichlorvos in comparison to the reactor with titanium dioxide coated on the inner surface operating at 360 nm. Furthermore, this study concluded that the rate of dichlorvos degradation was reduced in the presence of hydrogen peroxide.
